city: Lenovo US Fulfillment Center, Whitsett, NC
location: Lenovo US Fulfillment Center, Whitsett, NC
requirements: Location : Lenovo US Fulfillment Center, Whitsett, NC Schedule : Full-time, 5 days onsite per week, First Shift Operations Availability : Occasional weekends and holidays as required About Lenovo Lenovo is a global technology leader known for innovation in PCs, servers, mobile devices, and smart solutions. You can be part of a global leader in technology and innovation, working on cutting-edge server platforms and manufacturing systems while collaborating with top talent across engineering, logistics, and operations and enjoying a dynamic work culture with opportunities for growth and impact. At our US Fulfillment Center in Whitsett, NC, we drive excellence in manufacturing and logistics to support our Infrastructure Solutions Group (ISG). We are seeking a highly experienced and technically skilled Server Test Engineer to lead and optimize server production operations. This role is critical to ensuring high-quality output, process efficiency, and alignment with Lenovo’s global manufacturing standards. Key Responsibilities: Conduct comprehensive testing and validation of server hardware components. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure product quality and reliability. Develop and execute test plans, test cases, and test scripts to Lenovo global standards. Identify, document, and track defects, and work with engineering teams to resolve issues. Participate in design and specification reviews. Analyze test results and provide detailed reports to stakeholders. Able to setup test infrastructure or hardware like Core servers, switches, rack cabling. Troubleshooting any test related issues using python, shell or bash command line. Basic Qualifications: BS in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or a related field. Minimum of 3 years of experience in testing server hardware. Preferred Qualifications: Familiar with x86 Intel server architecture and/or GPU Nvidia Arm based technology. Familiarity with server components like CPU, memory, BIOS/UEFI, BMC, PSU, motherboard. Deep understanding of BIOS/UEFI and BMC validation, including Redfish, PLDM, IPMI. Proficiency with Tier 1 server OSes such as Windows Server, RHEL/SUSE, VMware. Proven ability to troubleshoot complex hardware issues. Hands on experience with Python and Bash scripting. Experience working in fast-paced manufacturing or production environments. Basic skills in IP, routing, MAC address handling, and port troubleshooting. Ability to configure PXE, DHCP, and other server services. Strong verbal and written English skills; effective team collaboration. Understanding of validation processes and test methodologies. Work Environment: Fast-paced, collaborative, and innovative environment. Emphasis on safety, sustainability, and continuous improvement. In essence, Test Engineers are the bridge between product design and the finished product, ensuring that manufacturing processes are efficient, cost-effective, and produce high-quality goods. The ideal candidate should demonstrate the ability to work independently in a high-pressure, results-oriented organization and have experience in self-management and motivating cross-functional teams to achieve business KPIs. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Additional
responsibilities: Conduct comprehensive testing and validation of server hardware components. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure product quality and reliability. Develop and execute test plans, test cases, and test scripts to Lenovo global standards. Identify, document, and track defects, and work with engineering teams to resolve issues. Participate in design and specification reviews. Analyze test results and provide detailed reports to stakeholders. Able to setup test infrastructure or hardware like Core servers, switches, rack cabling. Troubleshooting any test related issues using python, shell or bash command line. Basic

requirements: Lenovo ISG is seeking UEFI engineers with 6-15 yrs of experience for next generation server products. You will work closely with internal teams and be responsible for all UEFI/BIOS related works during the planning, development, and validation phases of the server product development. Responsibilities (but not limited as list): * Responsible for x86 and ARM server UEFI/BIOS development, including feature development and bug fixing. * Working with internal team to design new product features into next generation. * Working with internal team and end-customers to understand customer’s requirements. Requirements * Bachelors or higher degree in Computer Science, Electronics Engineering, or similar field. * Strong Server UEFI/ BIOS development experience. * Strong C coding skills. * Good knowledge on processor architecture and OS internals * Good knowledge one or more of the UEFI related technology areas like SMBIOS, Boot Loaders, PCIe, Secure Boot, System Performance etc. * Excellent debugging skills, experience in using debugging tools * Strong communication and collaboration skills with multi-functional teams. * Demonstrated ability in mentoring/developing junior staff members * Provides advice in technical/operational domain of specialization. Additional

requirements: Objetivo do Cargo: Atuar no suporte aos processos de vendas, incluindo elaboração de cotações e gestão das demandas operacionais da área, garantindo o tratamento e acompanhamento ponta a ponta das solicitações recebidas, com foco na resolução de problemas, alinhamento entre áreas e excelência na experiência dos clientes internos e externos. A posição requer perfil organizado, proativo e colaborativo, com capacidade de analisar situações, tratar exceções e atuar em conjunto com diferentes áreas da companhia, contribuindo para a eficiência da operação e para a melhoria contínua dos processos. Principais Responsabilidades Apoiar atividades relacionadas à elaboração de cotações, garantindo qualidade, precisão e cumprimento dos prazos estabelecidos. Atuar no suporte operacional aos processos de vendas, realizando análise, direcionamento e acompanhamento ponta a ponta das demandas da área, assegurando sua resolução dentro dos prazos e padrões estabelecidos pela companhia. Tratar exceções e apoiar a resolução de problemas relacionados ao ciclo de vendas, atuando em conjunto com as áreas responsáveis para identificar causas, viabilizar soluções e garantir a continuidade das operações. Trabalhar de forma integrada com Vendas, Parceiros, Clientes e demais áreas internas da companhia, facilitando a comunicação, destravando demandas e escalando situações críticas quando necessário. Manter controles operacionais e utilizar ferramentas corporativas para acompanhamento das atividades, organização da operação e monitoramento dos resultados da área. Identificar gargalos, riscos e oportunidades de melhoria, contribuindo para a eficiência da operação, padronização das atividades e evolução contínua dos processos. Compartilhar conhecimento e boas práticas com a equipe, contribuindo para a organização da área e o desenvolvimento contínuo do time. Formação e Experiência Ensino superior cursando ou concluído em Administração, Processos Gerenciais, Gestão Comercial, Engenharia ou áreas correlatas. Experiência prévia em atividades de Sales Support, Backoffice Comercial, Order Management, Customer Service ou áreas administrativas relacionadas ao suporte às vendas. Conhecimentos Técnicos Excel intermediário. Familiaridade com Power BI, SAP, MS Dynamics, Power Apps ou ferramentas similares será considerada um diferencial. Idiomas Inglês intermediário (leitura e escrita) será considerado um diferencial. Additional

The 4P Visual and Accessories Manager is responsible for product portfolio definition, product pricing, promotion budget allocation and identifying internal/external opportunities to develop the marketing campaign plan. In this role you will need to work closely with Lenovo Operations, the marketing team and channel sales team cross functions. While working with Management team you will analyze and evaluate the market environment to support the business decisions. This also includes cooperation with the Operation Product team to define the product portfolio plan and manage the planning tool. You will develop business tools to monitor major KPI’s using these KPI’s to ensure that the right actions are taken in country on promotions, portfolio, channel and marketing drive. You work independently and take initiative on complex issues and manage the full forecast for the region down to product and country level. You monitor the execution of the forecast and drive for higher forecast accuracy moving forward as well as set target too at a global level and at family level, monitor on a weekly basis and take corrective action if needed. In this role you will: Drive initiatives to maximize mix, margin and overall product contribution to the portfolio & business Be responsible for building, implementing, & executing country’s product demand generation programs, which align to quarterly & annual financial goals Analyze market research, customer feedback, and campaign performance data to identify target markets, optimize marketing strategies, and measure campaign effectiveness. Evaluate performance against plan at appropriate intervals (e.g., daily, weekly) and instigate corrective actions to keep country on track to achieve LOB goals Provide relevant input to the annual & quarterly planning cycles by LOB key financials (Platform Mix, Units, AUR, AUM, Rev$, Margin$) Effectively manage Product Transitions with a view to maximizing revenue & profit Ensure accurate forecasting for relevant products; co-owner (with Sales) for country’s forecast Effectively differentiate Lenovo offerings from competitive alternatives and create distinct customer preference for Lenovo offerings. You will know when to bring elements of competitors' offerings or products into the equation to create the highest yielding deal for the customer and Lenovo Feedback competitive “price: spec” details to COE product/pricing team to help arrive at appropriate positioning and product messaging Develop customer-facing presentations tailored to different audience segments, emphasizing the product's value proposition. Collaborate with sales and marketing teams to develop launch strategies for different distribution channels. Position Requirements: Minimum 5+ years product and/or business management experience. Visuals & computer accessory product experience is advantageous. A degree equivalent – preferably in Business. Strong business / customer understanding, and comfortable with financial management and P&Ls Experience with technology products, ideally from IT industry Good analytical skills with high level of accuracy in data analysis and reacts with appropriate urgency to situations. Strong stakeholder influence and able to convince sales team. If you're passionate about delivering impact and driving results, we'd love to meet you. Apply now and let's create something exceptional together. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Additional

requirements: The Senior Sales Specialist - Infrastructure Solutions Group (ISG) is responsible for driving revenue growth by acquiring new customers and expanding Lenovo's footprint within existing Enterprise and Public Sector accounts. Working closely with Account Managers, Client Managers, Channel Partners, and Technical Sales teams, the successful candidate will identify, qualify, and close opportunities across Lenovo's Infrastructure Solutions portfolio, including servers, storage, hybrid cloud, AI solutions, and services. The role is a senior individual-contributor role which requires strong customer engagement skills, solution-selling capabilities, and the ability to manage opportunities throughout the entire sales cycle. What you'll own: • Acquiring new Enterprise and Public Sector customers and develop business opportunities across assigned accounts, and within the assigned territory. • Building and maintaining a healthy pipeline of Infrastructure Solutions Group (ISG) opportunities, managing them from opportunity identification through closure. • Identifying, qualifying, and pursuing opportunities across Lenovo's server, storage, hybrid cloud, AI, and services portfolio. • Achieving and exceeding assigned sales targets and business objectives. • Acting as a trusted advisor by understanding customer business challenges and aligning Lenovo solutions to their needs. • Supporting customer retention efforts through proactive account engagement and relationship management, ensuring high levels of customer satisfaction and responsiveness. • Coordinating with technical specialists, solution architects, services teams, finance, and channel partners to develop customer solutions. • Managing RFPs, tenders, commercial discussion, contract negotiations and procurement processes for enterprise and public sector customers. • Working closely with channel partners to generate incremental pipeline and accelerate business growth. • Maintaining accurate opportunity tracking, pipeline management, and forecasting within CRM systems. What You Bring: • Bachelor's degree in Business, Information Technology, Engineering, or a related discipline. • Minimum 10–15 years of experience in enterprise technology sales, business development, or account management with Enterprise and/or Public Sector customers within the Gulf region. • Experience selling IT infrastructure solutions, data center technologies, cloud solutions, or enterprise services with a proven track record of achieving and exceeding sales targets. • Knowledge of server, storage, networking, hybrid cloud, AI, and infrastructure solutions. Experience with consumption-based or as-a-service offerings is an advantage. • Experience collaborating with channel partners and distributors. • Strong consultative and solution-selling skills, relationship building, stakeholder management capabilities, excellent communication, presentation, and negotiation abilities. Ready to advance your career and contribute to something bigger? Submit your application today and let's build the future together. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Additional

Principais responsabilidades da posição Reportando-se ao Gerente de OM para Brasil, as principais responsabilidades são: Análise dos principais problemas que impedem o carregamento dos pedidos no sistema e interação com os diversos times Lenovo para prover soluções pro-ativas de ordens atrasadas e críticas – Vendas / Fiscal / Fulfillment / Logística. Análise de documentação para processos de retorno de material, visando o correto carregamento no sistema. Análise de carteira de cliente, com as corretas tratativas e comunicação de datas e eventuais impedimentos para o correto fluxo do pedido, com análise da causa raiz para os casos que não atenderam o indicador de cada cliente, com o objetivo de suportar relatórios gerenciais para apresentação de resultados. Liderança no controle dos processos de retorno em andamento, coordenando ações com as equipes dentro do fluxo dos pedidos. Suportar o time de inventário nas ações de consumo de itens em estoque. The North American Services Supply Chain organization is seeking a strong candidate for the role of NA Order Fulfillment Specialist. This role within the team is to support in-warranty parts fulfillment for the PC sector of the business. The ideal candidate would be well organized and comfortable with managing multiple tasks including part order backlog, providing ETAs, clean order activities, and responding to emails in a timely manner to support our internal and external customers. Day to Day Responsibilities include: · Manage daily backlog for in-warranty PC part orders. o Track incoming supply and manage ETAs to customers. o Find alternate sources of supply for hard to source parts. o Identify Incorrect part orders and provide the correct part to order o Highlight help needed parts and work with the Worldwide team to pull in orders with extended ETAs. o Respond to personal and group email box inquiries from the call centers and other internal customer regarding questions about backorders · Manage clean order activities to ensure timely parts order fulfillment. o Work with the 3PL to rectify any orders that did not flow correctly. o Work with the inventory team for any needed adjustments on discrepant material. Basic Qualifications: · Bachelor’s degree in supply chain, business, or related field is preferred but not required. · Intermediate to advanced skills with Excel, PowerPoint and Outlook required. Preferred Qualifications: · 1-3 years experience in supply chain or service-related field preferred. · Must have strong organizational skills and be detail oriented. · Be able to effectively communicate and lead discussions with internal & external teams. · Power BI skills preferred. We follow a friendly hybrid model with three days a week in the office—great for collaboration and connection! We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Additional

requirements: Service Management Office (SMO – Delivery) is an integral component of our Digital Workplace Solutions (DWS) Delivery portfolio. From a delivery perspective, we are committed to providing exceptional professional service quality, aligned with contractual commitments and consistently delivering a high standard of Customer Experience (CX). Role DWS Delivery is seeking a results-driven professional with strong leadership capabilities, a proven track record in continuous process improvement, and extensive experience in delivery and vendor management. This role is pivotal to the successful execution of Lenovo’s delivery of professional services. The ideal candidate will oversee end-to-end service delivery, ensuring operational excellence, cost efficiency, and an outstanding customer experience across LATAM markets. Responsibilities Manage the Service Management Office (SMO), monitoring, controlling, and executing operations to consistently meet service levels, timelines, cost targets, and quality standards. Serve as the primary escalation point for customers during the delivery phase, ensuring timely communication and effective issue resolution. Collaborate with cross-functional teams—including presales, sales, engineering, logistics, and support—to ensure seamless service integration and execution. Coordinate with vendors throughout the delivery process, ensuring performance, compliance, and alignment with Lenovo’s service standards. Monitor and report on key performance indicators (KPIs) related service delivery and customer satisfaction. Lead and manage the existing team to ensure operational success. Oversee team expansion or reduction in response to market and customer demands. Drive continuous improvement initiatives to enhance service efficiency, scalability, cost-effectiveness, and overall customer experience. Including also land and expand. Ensure full compliance with contractual obligations and service-level agreements (SLAs). Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Business, Information Technology, Supply Chain, or a related field (Master’s degree preferred). Minimum of 5 years of experience in service delivery, operations, or project management, preferably within the IT or technology sector. In-depth knowledge of device lifecycle services and fulfillment operations. Strong expertise in time and motion studies, as well as continuous improvement methodologies such as Lean and Six Sigma. Demonstrated ability to lead complex projects and manage cross-functional teams in a global environment. Excellent communication, problem-solving, and customer relationship management skills. Familiarity with ITIL frameworks and best practices is a plus. Preferred Attributes Strong customer-centric mindset with a demonstrated commitment to delivering high-quality service and support. Analytical and solution-oriented, with a proactive approach to identifying challenges and implementing effective resolutions. Highly adaptable and comfortable working in fast-paced, dynamic environments with shifting priorities and tight deadlines. Exhibit a high level of emotional intelligence, including strong self-awareness, empathy, and interpersonal skills that foster effective collaboration, conflict resolution, and leadership. Proficiency in English (spoken and written) is required; additional language skills are a plus. Proficiency in Spanish or Portuguese is required; fluency in both is highly desirable. Additional

requirements: Responsibilities: Designs and implements support features within servers to ensure easy maintenance, troubleshooting, and repair in the field. Collaborates with product segment (marketing) on including Serviceability and Supportability features in Marketing Requirements Documents (MRDs). Works with product development engineers on delivery of Serviceability and Supportability features during design and development of next generation products. Develops tools and mechanisms within server hardware and software that allow for easy monitoring, logging, diagnostics, and fault isolation Reviews new server designs to verify that serviceability features function as intended and meet RAS requirements before release. Analyzes reported field issues to identify patterns and areas where serviceability can be improved. Collaterals internally (with Development, Procurement, Product Engineering, and support agents) and externally (Technology and platform design partners) to identify, negotiate, and drive serviceability and supportability requirements. Reviews support tools and documents for completeness, cost effectiveness, and CX. Combines technical and business acumens to balance product support, service delivery efficiency, and Quality. Performs ROI analysis in support of technical recommendations. Head of Service Channels Job Summary This position serves as the leader of Lenovo Japan's eService domain, including Support Site, FAQ, Chat, AI, and other self-service channels. The role is responsible for overseeing day-to-day operations, developing strategic direction, and driving continuous improvement initiatives to enhance customer experience (CX) and maximize self-service resolution rates. By leveraging Voice of Customer (VOC) insights and key performance metrics, the Head of Service Channels will identify improvement opportunities and establish a data-driven continuous improvement cycle that enhances service quality while optimizing operational costs. In addition, this role acts as a strategic partner to the Services Director and the Services Leadership Team. The position is expected to translate business strategies and transformation agendas into actionable execution plans, while leading cross-functional initiatives across Japan, Asia Pacific (AP), and Global teams to deliver measurable business outcomes. Key Responsibilities 1. eService Operations Leadership Own and lead the overall performance of Lenovo Japan's eService ecosystem, including Support Site, FAQ, Chat, AI, and other self-service channels. Monitor and manage key service metrics, including service quality, customer experience (CX), self-service resolution rates, and cost efficiency. Drive continuous improvements through VOC analysis, web analytics, and customer behavior insights. Accelerate service innovation and digital transformation through effective AI adoption and automation initiatives. Manage external vendors and collaborate with internal stakeholders to ensure operational excellence. 2. Cross-Functional Transformation & Operational Excellence Lead business process standardization, optimization, and operational efficiency initiatives. Drive cross-functional programs and strategic initiatives across multiple organizations. Collaborate with AP and Global teams to identify, adopt, and implement best practices. Champion medium- and long-term transformation agendas that support organizational growth and business objectives. 3. Leadership Partnership & Strategic Execution Translate business strategies and organizational priorities into actionable plans and measurable outcomes. Support business planning, budgeting, Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs), and executive business reviews. Lead governance processes for strategic initiatives, including project tracking, risk management, and stakeholder alignment. Drive cross-regional projects involving Japan, AP, and Global teams. Provide data-driven insights and recommendations to support executive decision-making. Serve as a trusted advisor and strategic partner to the Services Director, contributing to overall organizational success and business performance. Qualifications Required Qualifications Proven management experience in service operations, business operations, strategic planning, or related fields. Demonstrated people management experience with a track record of leading and developing teams. Experience managing KPIs and leading operational improvement initiatives. Proven ability to lead and influence cross-functional projects and stakeholders. Experience translating business strategies and executive priorities into executable action plans. Strong communication, presentation, and stakeholder management skills. Experience working with Asia Pacific and/or Global teams in a matrixed organization. Business-level English proficiency. Preferred Qualifications Experience managing eService, web support, FAQs, chat services, or self-service channels. Experience in contact center operations and/or customer support organizations. Proven track record of leading customer experience (CX) improvement initiatives. Experience with AI solutions, chatbots, knowledge management systems, or digital service platforms. Experience leading digital transformation and business process reengineering programs. Industry experience within hardware manufacturing, technology, IT services, or eCommerce environments. Key Competencies Strategic Thinking & Business Acumen Customer-Centric Mindset Data-Driven Decision Making Leadership & Team Development Change Management & Transformation Leadership Cross-Cultural Collaboration Stakeholder Influence & Executive Communication Operational Excellence & Continuous Improvement Innovation & AI Adoption Leadership Additional

requirements: Position Summary We are seeking an experienced and highly motivated Advisory UEFI Firmware Engineer to lead the design and development of next-generation server BIOS/UEFI firmware. This role requires deep technical expertise in UEFI architecture, strong system-level debugging capability, and the ability to drive cross-functional technical decisions. Key Responsibilities Technical Leadership Lead architecture, design, and implementation of complex UEFI firmware features. Own one or more critical firmware domains, including : (1) Boot Flow (2) Memory Initialization (3) PCIe (4) ACPI (5) SMBIOS (6) Security Define firmware architecture and coding guidelines Review technical design proposals and provide architectural direction. Firmware Development Develop and maintain EDK II based BIOS/UEFI firmware. Drive new platform bring-up for Intel and AMD server platforms. Implement new firmware features while maintaining high code quality. Optimize boot performance and firmware reliability. Support firmware security features including Secure Boot, TPM, Measured Boot, and capsule update. Debug & Problem Solving Lead complex root cause analysis involving: (1) CPU initialization (2) Memory training (3) PCIe enumeration (4) Boot failures (5) Performance issues (6) Hardware/Firmware interaction Utilize debugging tools such as: (1) Intel ITP/DCI (2) AMD HDT (3) UART logs Minimum Requirements: 8+ years of experience. Additional

requirements: Job Summary We are seeking a motivated AI Engineer to join our team in developing innovative AI-driven solutions for enterprise customers, with a focus on the Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) domain. This role is ideal for recent graduates or professionals with up to three years of experience who are passionate about artificial intelligence, machine learning, and enterprise software. You will work closely with cross-functional teams to design, build, and deploy AI solutions that enhance product development processes and improve operational efficiency. Key Responsibilities Design, develop, and deploy AI and machine learning solutions for B2B customers, particularly within the Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) domain. Collaborate with product managers, software engineers, and business stakeholders to gather requirements and translate business needs into AI-driven solutions. Research, develop, and optimize AI algorithms and models to improve PLM processes, including: Bill of Materials (BOM) management Product change management Component search and sourcing Integrate AI capabilities into existing PLM platforms to improve productivity, reduce time-to-market, and enhance product quality. Analyze large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights that support product development and manufacturing decisions. Fine-tune, evaluate, and deploy Transformer-based large language models (LLMs) and other AI models for enterprise use cases. Stay current with emerging AI technologies, machine learning techniques, and PLM industry trends to continuously improve solutions. Contribute to the design of scalable, maintainable, and production-ready AI systems. Provide technical guidance and share knowledge with junior team members when appropriate. Participate in Agile/Scrum development activities, including sprint planning, code reviews, testing, and continuous improvement. Qualifications Education Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Data Science, or a related discipline. Master's degree is preferred. Fresh graduates and campus hires are encouraged to apply. Experience 0–3 years of experience in Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Data Science, or related fields. Experience developing AI solutions for enterprise or B2B applications is an advantage. Knowledge or experience in the Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) domain is a strong advantage. Technical Skills Strong understanding of Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, and Deep Learning concepts. Proficiency in one or more programming languages such as: Python Java C++ Experience with AI/ML frameworks such as: TensorFlow PyTorch Keras Familiarity with Transformer architectures and pre-trained language models, including: GPT BERT Other Transformer-based models Experience with model fine-tuning, inference, prompt engineering, or deployment is preferred. Knowledge of big data technologies such as Hadoop and Spark. Experience working with SQL and NoSQL databases. Exposure to cloud platforms such as AWS, Microsoft Azure, or Google Cloud Platform is an advantage. Familiarity with Agile/Scrum software development methodologies. Required Competencies Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving abilities. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. Ability to explain technical concepts to both technical and non-technical audiences. Self-motivated with a willingness to learn new technologies. Ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a collaborative team. Passion for innovation and continuous improvement. Language Requirement Proficient in written and spoken English. This job description outlines the primary responsibilities and qualifications for the position. Duties and requirements may be modified from time to time to meet business needs and organizational objectives. Additional

requirements: We are looking for a Strategy & Operations Assistant to support CPC & SMB business operations through AI-enabled data processing, Power BI reporting, business analysis support, and daily department coordination. This role is designed for someone who is highly organized, data-sensitive, and confident in applying AI tools and Power BI to improve reporting efficiency, structure business information, visualize key insights, and support operational decision-making. The role will also provide administrative and executive support to ensure smooth daily operations for the team. Job Responsibilities 1、AI, Power BI & Data Operations（70%） --Apply AI tools to support information collection, data organization, content structuring, reporting, presentation preparation, and workflow efficiency improvement. --Use Power BI / PBI to organize, analyze, visualize, and maintain business and operational data. --Build and update dashboards, trackers, business reports, and management review materials. --Consolidate data from multiple sources and translate findings into clear, structured insights for business reviews and decision-making. --Support regular business updates, operational summaries, performance tracking, and presentation materials. --Identify opportunities to improve team efficiency through AI tools, templates, automation, and digital workflows. --Explore practical use cases for AI in business operations, reporting, data analysis, and team productivity improvement. 2、Administrative & Executive Support（30%） --Act as the key administrative contact for CPC & SMB operational matters. --Support the execution of key operational initiatives across CPC & SMB teams. --Lead the planning, coordination, and execution of internal events and department activities. --Provide executive support, including calendar management, travel arrangements, meeting coordination, expense processing, and daily administrative tasks. Job Qualifications --Bachelor’s degree or above in Data Analytics, Information Systems, Business, or related fields. --Hands-on experience using AI tools in real work scenarios. --Hands-on experience with Power BI / PBI, Excel, PowerPoint, and other reporting or data visualization tools. --Good understanding of business intelligence, data analysis, reporting, and digital productivity solutions. --Strong analytical thinking, attention to detail, and ability to structure information clearly. --Strong communication and coordination skills, with the ability to work across teams and functions. --Good English and Chinese communication skills, both written and verbal. --Proactive, responsible, well-organized, and able to manage multiple tasks with clear priorities. Additional

requirements: 【岗位职责】 1、负责 MCU 产品软件架构的整体规划、设计、落地与版本迭代； 2、负责MCU芯片平台、RTOS系统、通信协议、存储方案、升级方案、低功耗方案的选型与论证； 3、负责解决MCU产品研发、测试、量产阶段的各类核心疑难问题，包括系统性死机、随机复位、高低温异常、EMC兼容问题、功耗超标、通信丢包、内存溢出、系统调度异常等技术难点； 4、负责产品架构级性能与功耗优化，统筹系统调度优化、任务优先级设计、内存管理优化、IO吞吐优化，解决产品卡顿、响应延迟等问题； 【任职要求】 1、本科及以上学历，电子信息、自动化、嵌入式、计算机相关专业，5年以上 MCU 嵌入式开发经验及1年以上架构设计经验； 2、熟练使用主流单片机平台，如STM32、GD32、ESP32、NXP、TI 等，熟悉标准库、HAL库、LL库开发； 3、熟悉 I2C、UART、SPI等通信协议； 4、熟悉单片机、ARM及其外围电路驱动设计； 5、熟悉蓝牙BLE、WiFi等无线通信开发，加分项：精通MCU低功耗架构、固件OTA升级架构设计； 6、良好的英文读写能力，加分项：口语交流能力 Additional
